metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5161
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4, 10: immunogold-EM of the hippocampus and amygdala showed FAAH (fatty-acid amide hydrolase) localization to dendritic spines but not presynaptic terminals.

Figure 12: immunogold-EM of the cerebellum showed (a minor subpopulation of) FAAH labelled axon terminals that formed synapses on FAAH-negative somata and dendrites.

Figure 3: FAAH was not detectable in GABAergic interneurons.

Figure 1: FAAH antibody specificity was verified by immunostaining hippocampal sections from FAAH-KO mice.

Authors discussed the overall localization of FAAH;
"As expected from its structure, FAAH may be located on the cytoplasmic surface of membranes. However, quite unexpectedly, the enzyme was primarily associated with intracellular membranes, including smooth endoplasmic reticulum cisternae and the external surface of the mitochondria, but not with the plasma membrane.". Experimental description: Both mice and rats were used. Assume rat was used in the EM localizations, as the title of the paper states: "Segregation of two endocannabinoid-hydrolyzing enzymes into pre- and postsynaptic compartments in the rat hippocampus, cerebellum and amygdala"

antibody:
"Two antibodies were used to visualize the distribution of FAAH at the cellular and subcellular levels. Anti-FAAH was raised against FAAH-GST fusion protein, while the anti-FAAH-ΔTM was raised against a native, 6X-His tagged truncation of FAAH. Staining with the two antibodies resulted in identical labelling pattern, as shown in Fig. 1 in the hippocampus. The specificity of the FAAH antibodies was verified by immunostaining hippocampal sections from FAAH-KO mice"
